target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B AI & Software company with a current post-money valuation of $1.0B and $155M raised to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with the private set including Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Two methods drive the triangulation. Stage-progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier from the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.9B at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $5.3B, though the range is wide ($500M to $32.0B), reflecting substantial dispersion in frontier AI valuations. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively on observed mark-ups, while peer-median captures broader market pricing across the cohort.

The triangulated range spans $1.6B (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects material uncertainty inherent in the peer set: Safe Superintelligence trades at a significant premium, while Photoroom trades at a substantial discount to Nominal's current valuation. Key sensitivities include the applicability of each peer's stage and founding context to Nominal's specific profile, and whether frontier AI companies command durably elevated multiples. The current $1.0B valuation sits at the lower quartile of the implied range.
